title: A Multi-Agent Large Language Model Framework for Emergency Treatment Recommendation in Acute Ischemic Stroke: Development and Validation Study
authors_text: Yuehua Li, Bi-Cong Yan, Ruipeng Zhang, Xinyu Song, Li Chen, Zhongzheng Cao.
abstract: Acute ischemic stroke requires rapid, guideline-driven treatment decisions within narrow time windows. This study presents a multi-agent large language model framework that orchestrates specialized agents for imaging interpretation, clinical feature extraction, and treatment recommendation, supporting real-world clinical decision-making in acute ischemic stroke. Developed and validated on multicenter data, the framework demonstrates the feasibility of agentic LLM systems for time-critical emergency stroke workflows.
